name: BleepingComputer
description: Breaking breach news, ransomware leaks, and malware analysis — fast wire coverage.

name: Krebs on Security
description: Brian Krebs' original investigative security reporting — the gold standard for attribution and follow-the-money analysis.

name: The Hacker News
description: High-volume daily security news aggregator covering CVEs, breaches, and disclosures.

name: Dark Reading
description: Enterprise-focused security analysis aimed at CISOs and SOC leaders.

name: CyberSecurity News
description: Low-authority aggregator that re-writes content from BleepingComputer and The Hacker News.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — duplicates higher-authority primary sources.

name: SecurityWeek
description: Major independent cybersecurity outlet covering breaches, vulnerabilities, and policy.

name: Help Net Security
description: Predominantly vendor surveys, product roundups, and press releases.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — product/survey content outweighed incident reporting.

name: Schneier on Security
description: Bruce Schneier's long-running blog on cryptography, security policy, and privacy — essential for big-picture analysis.

name: Black Hills Information Security
description: Practitioner-level pentest, threat hunting, and active defense content from the BHIS team.

name: Hacker News
description: General tech community feed — off-topic for a security-focused newsroom.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — majority of items are general tech, not security.

name: CISA Alerts
description: Official U.S. CISA cybersecurity alerts and advisories — authoritative primary source.

name: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities
description: CISA's KEV catalog — the authoritative list of actively exploited vulnerabilities requiring immediate remediation.

name: SANS Internet Storm Center
description: Daily threat analysis and incident diaries from SANS ISC handlers — strong practitioner signal.

name: NCSC UK
description: UK National Cyber Security Centre advisories and threat reports — authoritative national-CERT primary source.

name: Cisco Talos Intelligence
description: Strong threat research, paused pending vendor-heavy-coverage review against independent sources.

name: Check Point Research
description: Vendor research arm publishing malware analysis and campaign reports.

name: Mandiant (Google Cloud)
description: World-class APT attribution research; paused pending feed-reliability verification.

name: Google Threat Analysis Group
description: Google's TAG on government-backed attacks and zero-day tracking — authoritative primary research.

name: Google Project Zero
description: Elite vulnerability research team publishing deep technical zero-day write-ups.

name: Microsoft Security Response Center
description: Authoritative Microsoft security disclosures; paused pending feed-format stability confirmation.

name: Microsoft Security Blog
description: Primarily product marketing interleaved with research.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — marketing content outweighed standalone research value.

name: CrowdStrike Blog
description: Vendor blog mixing threat research with sales and product marketing at roughly a 1:4 ratio.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — majority of items are commercial/sales content rather than research. Revisit via whitelist on /blog/threat-research/ if the ratio improves.

name: SentinelLabs
description: SentinelLabs research on malware families, threat actors, and exploitation.

name: Palo Alto Unit 42
description: In-depth APT, ransomware, cloud, and IoT research — one of the most cited vendor research outlets.

name: Sophos News
description: Strong X-Ops research; paused pending feed noise review (product posts interleaved with research).

name: Kaspersky Securelist
description: Kaspersky GReAT team's APT and crimeware research — widely cited primary source.

name: Malwarebytes Labs
description: Threat intelligence on malware, scams, and enterprise threats.

name: WeLiveSecurity (ESET)
description: ESET research on APT campaigns and Eastern European threats.

name: Rapid7 Blog
description: Emergent threat analysis and Metasploit-adjacent vulnerability research.

name: Qualys Threat Research
description: Patch Tuesday analysis and cloud posture findings that largely duplicate ZDI, Rapid7, and The Hacker News.
whyDrop: Signal redundancy — patch coverage already provided by higher-authority sources in our pool.

name: Zero Day Initiative (ZDI)
description: Trend Micro's ZDI advisories from their bug-bounty program — primary-source vulnerability disclosures.

name: Fortinet FortiGuard Labs
description: Vendor patch summaries that typically lack CVE detail and independent analysis.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — contributed to the April 2026 CVE-hedging ship quality issue; patch advisories without identifiers downstream.

name: Proofpoint Threat Insight
description: Mix of threat research with webinar and analyst-report promotions.
whyDrop: Signal-to-noise threshold — promotional content interleaved with research reduced the usable yield.

name: Elastic Security Labs
description: Malware analysis, detection engineering, and threat hunting techniques.

name: Recorded Future (Insikt Group)
description: Nation-state threat research and geopolitical cyber risk analysis.

name: The Record (Recorded Future News)
description: Independent cybersecurity journalism covering nation-state operations, ransomware, and policy — staffed by career security reporters.

name: Volexity Threat Research
description: Small independent research shop with a consistent track record of zero-day discoveries (Ivanti, Atlassian, Exchange).

name: Censys Research
description: Internet-scan-based research on exposed infrastructure, IOC enrichment, and attack-surface trends.

name: NVD — Recent CVEs
description: NIST NVD recent-CVE JSON feed — authoritative source-of-truth for the vulnerabilities category's CVE hard-gate.
whyDrop: Parser for the NVD JSON schema is not yet built in this pipeline (type `nvd-json` is a placeholder). Enable once the parser lands.

name: FreeBuf (ZH-native)
description: Chinese-language security community — first native ZH source added to the pool; enters as ingest-only per the contract spec for a 30-day audit before promotion.
